What “scene” are you a part of? d12 HOW DOES YOUR CHARACTER FEEL ABOUT THE MALL? 01Things are looking up. 02 People are starting to notice the effort you are putting in. 03Your talent is being wasted. 04Is my shift over yet? 05 This place is the root of your problems. 06You cannot escape this place. 07 Some of the people you care about most work here. 08 The other people at the mall are the worst. 09When you’re here, you’re family. 10A job’s a job. 11 Things are going to turn around soon. 12This place is your prison. Character Creation // Once the barrier goes up, money holds little worth. [33] ▶Choose 2 items your character brought to the mall (or roll once on each table). ▶Employee lockers are in the Break Room (p. 16). Employees mark what item(s) are there at the start of play. ▶Most mall visitors do not have weapons larger than a knife (d6) or taser/mace (DEX save or momentarily stunned). The table below provides examples. More are included in the core rules. STEP 4: STARTING GEAR d20EQUIPMENT TABLE 1 01Leather Jacket 02Spray Paint Cans 03Polaroid Camera 04Skateboard 05Roller Skates 06Boombox 07Six-Pack 08Gum 09Lighter 10Nail Polish Remover 11Paint Pens 12Lock Pick Set 13Purchase From Swords & Stuff 14Cosplay 15Scene-Appropriate Attire 16Pack of Cigarettes 17Taser (d6 targets DEX) 18DIY Tattoo Kit 19Pet 20Instrument d20EQUIPMENT TABLE 2 01Knife (d6) 02Music Player & Headphones 03Mouth Spray 04Workout Equipment 05Remote Control Car 06Key Wallet 07JNCO Jeans 08Walkie Talkies 09Tool(s) 10Handcuffs 11Bundle of Nylon Rope 12Letter of Resignation 13A Fifth 14Shirt, Tie, Slacks 15Shoplifted Item 16Civilian Clothes 17Lacrosse Stick 18Effigy 19Stolen key to a shop 20Gun (d6) [34] Character Creation // Making NPCs leads to the best moments in play. ▶ Age: Choose or roll 1d12+17. ▶ Look: Choose a style of clothing or look for your character. �Employees: What’s your uniform? Did you bring a change of clothes? ▶ Name: What is your character’s name? STEP 5: GENERAL INFO What rumor did your PC hear recently? STEP 7: RUMORS STOP! This step is completed as a party/table. CONNECTIONS Choose/create an NPC that your character has a connection with. ▶Describe them and their connection to you. BONDS Describe the bond your PC has with another PC. STEP 6: ENTANGLEMENTS d20 EXAMPLE BONDS 01Crush11Lent Money 02Dating12Annoyed by 03 Ex (friend or lover) 13 Helped out of a bind. 04 Work Proximity Associate 14 Wish Was Friend 05 Obsessed With 15Parental Figure 06Rival16Mentor 07 (Vice) Buddy 17Work Spouse 08Confidant18Co-Worker 09Enemy19Supervisor 10Frenemy20Friend d12RUMORS 01 The disappearances in town have something to do with the mall. 02 Terry has a bunch of contraband hidden in security. 03 If you can get into the vents, you can get anywhere in the mall. 04 Billy (Mr. Neil’s assistant) is living off of the loading dock 05 Mr. Neil has been tracking mud all over the mall. 06 There are hidden cameras in the mall. 07Maggie carries a gun. 08 The Mall Rats sneak into the mall at night. 09Emmy's weapons are real. 10 Joe’s Burger Barn is closed today because Joe’s being investigated. Change This Module // Nothing is sacred here, change it to fit your needs! [35] WAYS TO CHANGE THIS MODULE ▶Directly connect Your Body Is a Temple (p. 14) to the weird. They chose this mall based on the same type of research Mr. Neil was doing. They want to harness the thing and its portal to bring about the reign of their own "god." ▶Introduce an NPC directly connected to the weird. Perhaps a cynical, heavy-smoking magic- wielding detective who came to investigate the temporal shifts beneath the mall. ▶Increase (or decrease) the number of “You've Been REPLACED” Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶Make The REPLACED(p. 8) more distinct in their odd behavior (introducing Invasion of the Body Snatchers as a touchstone). ▶Increase the population of the mall and lean heavily into faction play. ▶Introduce well-equipped Bureau Agents who will prioritize containing or extracting any paranatural assets, with a secondary mission to keep knowledge of this event from spreading. AGENTS OF THE O.D.D. Framing The O.D.D. has intel that an oddity is being used in a highly populated location. It requires immediate containment. Agents have been dispatched to extract the Arcana and ensure a quick solution to the ever evolving situation. Ability Scores CTRL can be REPLACED directly with WIL. Embracing Horror Whisper Cards can be used as an optional mechanic, but if the table does not want to have Agents run the risk of being REPLACED, instead lean heavily into Liminal Horror’s inventory and Fallout systems. THE COMPANY Framing The WBC has sent agents to recruit (or neutralize) potential employee #758B (Logan D of the Carpenter DMV). On route they received word of a potential Asset being deployed at the same mall. The Asset Recovery and Containment Division (ARC) is now tasked with recovery of any potential resources. Standard information suppression protocol is also in place. Stress Translates directly. Armor & Stability Armor or Stability instead adds to BODY. Fallout If a character rolls on the Burnout Effects they also receive a Fallout (p.x). Fallout Stat increases map to the skill that makes the most sense. Enemy Conversion: ▶ Health: A Variable’s Health equals HP divide by 2 ▶ Aware: A Variable’s Aware is 1 if DEX is 1-10 2 if DEX is11-14, 3 if DEX is 15+. ▶ Combat: A Variable’s Combat is based on damage dice: 1(d4), 2(d6), 3(d8), 4(d10+) The following conversion guide advises on how to use this module with systems other than Liminal Horror. In play, use the chosen system’s character generation, rules and equipment stats. CONVERSION GUIDES [36] Conversion Guides // Liminal Horror adventures can adapt to a myriad of systems. MONSTER OF THE WEEK Framing There is something brewing at The Carpenter Mall. You’ve never been here personally, but a reliable contact swears by it. People have been
